question:
Identify a LAMMPS-compatible carbon interatomic potential with native uncertainty quantification (predictive variance) so I can:
- compute graphene phonon dispersion with error bars, and
- detect out-of-training (OOD) structures during an NPT MD run.

Which specific carbon model should I use (e.g., GAP/QUIP carbon such as the Deringer–Csányi GAP-C or a Bayesian ACE model), which LAMMPS pair_style enables it, and how do I output per-atom/force uncertainty (GP variance/sigma) for OOD monitoring? Include recommended model file names/links and example LAMMPS input settings to enable and dump uncertainties.

Identify the best interatomic potential (or intralayer+interlayer combination) for classical MD of multilayer graphene with vacancies that can accurately predict both lattice thermal conductivity and interlayer friction. The potential must (1) capture in-plane sp2 C–C bonding, bond breaking/reconstruction around vacancies, and phonon dispersion relevant to thermal transport; (2) include registry-dependent van der Waals interlayer forces to reproduce interlayer binding, AB/AA energy corrugation, sliding barriers, and shear; and (3) be available in LAMMPS. Compare and justify among AIREBO/AIREBO-M/REBO, optimized Tersoff, LCBOPII, ReaxFF, and hybrid schemes that pair an intralayer carbon potential (e.g., REBO or AIREBO-M) with a registry-dependent interlayer model such as Kolmogorov–Crespi (KC), DRIP, or ILP. Prioritize options validated against DFT/experiment for vacancy energetics, graphene thermal conductivity, and interlayer friction, and recommend the most appropriate choice.

Identify the specific carbon–copper (C–Cu) interatomic potential that was explicitly developed for molecular dynamics of graphene CVD on Cu at temperatures above copper’s experimental melting point without melting the Cu substrate. Provide the potential’s name/type (e.g., bond-order/Morse/MEAM/ReaxFF), the original citation (authors, year, journal), and any common LAMMPS implementation or parameter file names.

For MD/MC simulations of the diamond↔graphite phase transformation where accurate long-range dispersion is required to bind graphite layers, which carbon interatomic potential is recommended (e.g., LCBOP/LCBOPII, AIREBO/AIREBO-M, ReaxFF variants, ML potentials like GAP), and why? Please compare their ability to:
- Reproduce graphite interlayer spacing (~3.35 Å) and exfoliation energy (~0.32–0.36 J/m^2)
- Capture sp2↔sp3 rehybridization, bond breaking/formation, and phase stability across T–P
- Predict transformation pathways/barriers and elastic/phonon properties
Include known limitations, validated parameter sets, benchmark references, and LAMMPS/MC compatibility; exclude potentials without explicit vdW terms (e.g., plain Tersoff/REBO) unless combined with an interlayer potential.

Identify interatomic potentials for Pd–C that are validated for modeling the adsorption/adhesion of 2–5 nm Pd nanoclusters (icosahedral, decahedral, cuboctahedral) on monolayer graphene in large-scale classical MD (LAMMPS). Prioritize non-reactive, computationally efficient models compatible with Pd–Pd EAM/MEAM and C–C AIREBO/LCBOP/Tersoff, but also include reactive options. For each candidate (e.g., Pd/C/H/O ReaxFF, COMB3, MEAM with Pd–C cross terms, or Morse/LJ Pd–C fits to DFT), provide: parameter sets and LAMMPS pair_style compatibility; validation against DFT/experiment for Pd/graphene (binding/adhesion energy, equilibrium separation, registry dependence, wetting behavior) in the 300–800 K range; known limitations (over/underbinding, lack of charge transfer/dispersion, spurious carbide formation); and links to parameter files or key references.

Identify the interatomic potential (and exact parameter set) for Ti–Al–C that best reproduces Ti3AlC2 (312 MAX phase) deformation mechanisms—basal slip on (0001)<11-20>, kink-band formation under compression, and strong plastic anisotropy—in molecular dynamics. Prefer parameterizations validated against DFT/experiment for lattice constants, elastic constants, generalized stacking-fault (GSF) energies on (0001), and dislocation behavior. Specify the recommended potential family (e.g., 2NN-MEAM, MEAM, ReaxFF, machine-learning potential), the authors/year (e.g., Ti–Al–C 2NN-MEAM by Lee/Baskes; ReaxFF Ti/Al/C sets; MLIP/GAP/SNAP for MAX phases), LAMMPS file names if available, and key citations demonstrating correct basal slip and kink-band reproduction.

Which interatomic potential parameterization is best for classical MD simulations of irradiation damage in Ti3AlC2 (MAX phase)? Specifically, identify Ti–Al–C potentials (MEAM/COMB3/ReaxFF/BOP, etc.) that:
- Are parameterized for Ti3AlC2 and the full Ti–Al–C interactions
- Include ZBL short-range splining for high-energy collisions (PKA ≈ 1–50 keV)
- Reproduce DFT-calibrated defect properties (vacancy/interstitial/antisite formation energies on Ti/Al/C sublattices, threshold displacement energies, key migration barriers)
- Have been validated in displacement-cascade or radiation-tolerance studies (300–1000 K)
Please provide citations and, if available, LAMMPS-ready parameter files.

For classical MD (LAMMPS) simulations of thermal transport across lateral graphene/h‑BN stripe superlattices and embedded h‑BN dots in graphene (comparing zigzag vs armchair interfaces), which Tersoff potential provides reliable B–C and N–C cross interactions? Please recommend a specific, validated BCN Tersoff parameter set for phonons/thermal transport at graphene–hBN interfaces (reference and LAMMPS file name), and clarify whether I should use a unified B–C–N fit (e.g., Kinaci–Haskins–Sevik–Çağin 2012, BNC.tersoff) versus mixing a carbon-only set (e.g., Lindsay–Broido) with a BN set. Include any known caveats for edge/interface energies relevant to zigzag vs armchair comparisons.

Identify a machine-learning interatomic potential for the Fe–C system that is validated for bcc α-Fe with dilute interstitial carbon and suitable for finite-temperature MD of 1/2<111> screw-dislocation mobility, including C-decorated core structures. Prioritize models (e.g., GAP, MTP, ACE, SNAP, DP, HDNNP) trained to DFT on Fe–C configurations relevant to dislocations (core structures, C binding/migration near cores, point defects) rather than on pure carbon phases, with demonstrated accuracy for Peierls barrier, kink-pair energetics, and C–dislocation drag, and with ready-to-use implementations in LAMMPS/QUIP/DeePMD. Provide specific candidate potentials, their training set scope, validation on dislocations, and links/DOIs for download.

Which specific hybrid Fe–C EAM/ADP potential should I use in LAMMPS to model carbon-induced strengthening in bcc iron that (i) reproduces the compact, non-degenerate 1/2<111> screw dislocation core, (ii) yields a realistic Peierls barrier/Peierls stress (~0.9–1.1 GPa) for screw glide, and (iii) matches DFT-calibrated C solution, migration, and C–screw binding energies? Please identify the recommended potential (e.g., the Itakura Fe ADP combined with Hepburn–Ackland Fe–C as used by Maresca & Curtin), include the key citation(s), and provide the LAMMPS potential file name/source location.

Identify an Fe–C MEAM (preferably 2NN‑MEAM) interatomic potential specifically validated for theta‑Fe3C (cementite, Pnma) that accurately reproduces:
- elastic constants Cij at 0–300 K,
- low‑index surface energies (e.g., {010}, {100}, {001}),
- formation enthalpy of Fe3C,
and maintains Fe3C stability to enable a two‑phase coexistence melting/decomposition simulation. Provide the recommended parameterization (authors/year/DOI), where to obtain LAMMPS meam/meam/c parameter files, and summarize published benchmarks versus experiment/DFT for these properties. Note any known limitations (e.g., cementite high‑T stability, incongruent melting, graphite/diamond energetics) that could affect elastic constants, surfaces, or two‑phase MD results.

Identify publicly available Atomic Cluster Expansion (ACE) potentials compatible with LAMMPS (pair_style pace) for reactive C/H systems suitable for shock MD of methane, benzene, and condensed carbon up to ~100 GPa and 3000–8000 K. Specifically:
- Name the CH and C-only ACE parameterizations (file names/repo links) trained on high-P/T DFT data (compressed solids, liquids, dissociated hydrocarbons; graphite/diamond/melt) and validated for EOS/Hugoniot or phase transitions.
- Provide their basis settings (nu_max, l_max, n_max), cutoff, inclusion of long-range terms, and reported accuracy/stability in nonequilibrium NVE shock runs.
- Note performance/scalability in LAMMPS for 1e5–1e6 atoms.
If no ACE model meets these high-P/T reactive requirements, state that explicitly and recommend the closest available ACE (e.g., pure carbon) and best-practice guidance or datasets to train a CH ACE for shocks with PACE.

Which specific ReaxFF parameterization (citation and ffield filename) is recommended for LAMMPS reax/c simulations of graphene, carbon nanotubes, and fullerenes in C/H/O/N environments that has published validation for sp2-carbon properties—graphene phonon dispersion (ZA/TA/LA), in-plane elastic constants, and tensile fracture behavior—while supporting reactions with H, O, and N? Please note if the ReaxFF-lg (long-range vdW) variant is required for layered/curved carbon and any known limitations of common CHO/CHON sets (e.g., Chenoweth 2008 CHO, Mueller 2010 CH, later CHON updates).

For classical MD of fracture and fatigue (bond scission, crack growth under cyclic loading) in saturated hydrocarbon polymers such as polyethylene, which reactive interatomic potential is most appropriate when only sp3 C–C/C–H chemistry must be accurate (no need for unsaturated hydrocarbons or graphite/diamond)? Please compare AIREBO/REBO/AIREBO-M vs ReaxFF variants in terms of validation for alkanes/polyethylene, ability to model bond breaking/formation and van der Waals/torsions, known limitations in fracture mechanics, computational cost, and LAMMPS support (pair_style names and recommended parameter files).

Which Si–C interatomic potential is recommended for MD simulations of radiation damage in cubic β-SiC (3C-SiC) that require realistic short-range repulsion and accurate threshold displacement energies? Please identify a parameterization that:
- Implements an explicit ZBL (Ziegler–Biersack–Littmark) repulsive correction via a smooth spline/merge at short separations (~0.5–1.2 Å),
- Reproduces Ed values close to experiment/DFT (Ed_C ≈ 18–25 eV, Ed_Si ≈ 30–40 eV),
- Has been validated in displacement-cascade simulations,
and provide the canonical citation and (if available) LAMMPS files. Examples to consider include Erhart–Albe ABOP (2005), Gao–Weber Tersoff–ZBL (1997), Justo et al. EDIP–ZBL (2000/2001), and 2NN-MEAM–ZBL (Lee–Baskes).
